title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: A joint UN-EU-World Bank assessment published April 21 found Gaza needs $71.4 billion over a decade for recovery — a 77-year human development setback — with $35.2 billion in physical damage and 371,888+ housing units destroyed or damaged. The IDF eliminated two Hamas operatives in central Gaza: Anas Khaled Safi (planning attacks on IDF) and Ayman Housna (weapons manufacturer). Trump's Board of Peace held talks with UAE's DP World on Gaza supply chain and port logistics, though DP World denied awareness of formal discussions. Econ data updated to reflect the $71.4B reconstruction cost figure.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: Hamas missed a second Board of Peace disarmament deadline on April 19 and proposed handing over only police/internal security weapons — explicitly excluding the Qassam Brigades' full military arsenal. The Board of Peace's eight-month plan text was published on April 20, requiring Hamas to surrender rockets, drones, and tunnel maps within 90 days; Hamas countered with a three-year alternative. Cairo backchannel contacts continue. IDF strikes on April 15 killed six Palestinians including two children. Claims updated with April 19–20 negotiations details; Aryeh Lightstone (US senior adviser) added to political profiles.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: Hamas Signals Limited Police Weapons Handover; Qassam Brigades Military Arsenal Explicitly Excluded. Hamas Misses Second Board of Peace Disarmament Deadline; Cairo Talks Continue With No Breakthrough.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: Hamas-US Cairo talks collapsed without tangible progress on April 17; Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan on April 14. The IDF killed three Hamas commanders including a Nukhba unit participant from the October 7 hostage-taking from Kibbutz Re'im. Six months into the Gaza ceasefire, NPR and Al Jazeera documented that recovery has not begun — less than $1B of $17B pledged reconstruction funds received. The IDF states it expects Hamas to 'soon enter the disarmament process' despite Hamas's categorical rejection. Political profiles updated to include Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas Gaza chief who led the April 17 Cairo delegation.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: On April 17, 2026, direct negotiations between Hamas representatives and US officials in Cairo collapsed without advancing the second phase of the Gaza ceasefire agreement. On April 17, 2026, Al Jazeera documented that Israeli attacks had killed several Palestinians over two days in Gaza despite the ongoing October 2025 ceasefire agreement.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: On April 16, 2026, the IDF announced it had killed three Hamas commanders in separate targeted strikes in Gaza during the prior week. On April 16, 2026 — marking approximately six months since the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire — NPR published a major assessment documenting the lack of meaningful recovery in Gaza despite the truce.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: On April 15, 2026, Israeli strikes killed at least six Palestinians in Gaza, including two children. IDF Documents Ceasefire Violation: Palestinian Crossed Yellow Line Posing 'Immediate Threat'.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: On April 14, 2026 — the extended Tuesday deadline set by the Board of Peace for Hamas to formally accept or reject its phased disarmament framework — Hamas rejected the disarmament clause outright.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Mon Apr 13, 2026
summary: Qatar Begins Gaza Debris Removal on Al-Wahda Street Despite Disarmament Stalemate.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sun Apr 12, 2026
summary: UN Security Council Calls for Gaza Ceasefire Consolidation; Warns of Rising Violence.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: Hamas Submits Counterproposals on Disarmament as Saturday Deadline Passes Without Acceptance.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: Gaza Six-Month Ceasefire Assessment: 'Neither War Nor Peace'. Board of Peace Faces Severe Cash Crunch: Only $1B of $17B Pledged Received.

title: Breaking: Gaza Ceasefire 'On the Brink' as Hamas Disarmament Deadline Expires
summary: Gaza ceasefire described as 'on the brink' as Hamas disarmament deadline set to expire; deal collapse risk rises.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: On April 8, 2026, Snopes investigated viral claims that the US State Department had redirected $1.25 billion in existing funds to support Trump's 'Board of Peace' (BoP) Gaza reconstruction framework.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Sources report a firm deadline has been set for Hamas to respond to disarmament terms by the end of the week, significantly escalating international pressure on the group.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Three new daily event files created for April 3, 5, and 6, 2026. Key developments: Hamas formally refused disarmament talks until Israel fulfills Phase 1 obligations; on April 5, al-Qassam Brigades spokesman Abu Obeida called disarmament demands 'not acceptable' and framed them as cover for continued genocide. On April 6, the IDF reported 139 total ceasefire violations since October 2025 (22 since Feb 28) and struck Hamas weapons smuggler Ali Ahmed Ali Amrain in central Gaza. US Senators sought a briefing from the FBI and DOJ on Joint Task Force October 7 accountability progress. Meta updated to day 912; heroSubtitle reflects latest disarmament standoff.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Hamas Military Wing: Disarmament Demands 'Not Acceptable' — Qassam Brigades Spokesman Speaks Out.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Hamas Refuses Disarmament Talks Until Israel Fulfills Phase 1 Obligations. US Senators Request Briefing on Joint Task Force October 7 Progress.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Six new daily event files were created covering March 28 – April 2, 2026. Key developments: Hamas has not formally responded to the Board of Peace disarmament proposal as Phase Two talks remain frozen. Israeli airstrikes on April 1 killed at least 32 Palestinians including 7 children, one of the deadliest post-ceasefire days. OCHA confirmed the cumulative Gaza death toll reached 72,265 as of March 25, 2026 with 171,959 injured. Three journalists were killed in an Israeli strike on a press vehicle in Lebanon on March 28. The meta.json and casualties totals were updated to reflect OCHA's March 27 situation report; UN High Representative Nickolay Mladenov was added to political profiles.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: Israeli airstrikes on April 1, 2026 killed at least 32 people including 7 children and injured more than 30 across Gaza. As of early April 2026, only 38,358 of 94,800 allocated trucks had entered Gaza since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire — just 40% of the agreed amount.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: An IDF airstrike on March 31, 2026 in Zawayda, central Gaza, killed a senior Gaza police official along with eight other people and injured 14. UN: Israel Approved Nearly 37,000 West Bank Settlement Units in Past 12 Months.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Prism Reports published an investigative piece on March 30, 2026 documenting that Israeli forces continued to target journalists in Gaza even after the ceasefire that took effect October 10, 2025. Netanyahu Orders Crackdown on Settler Violence in West Bank.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sun Mar 29, 2026
summary: The IDF announced on March 29, 2026 (Palm Sunday) that it struck and killed approximately 10 armed Hamas operatives in central Gaza overnight. US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz told the Security Council on March 25, 2026: 'The future of Gaza is entirely dependent now on Hamas decommissioning its weapons.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: As of March 28, 2026, Hamas confirmed it is formally reviewing the Board of Peace disarmament framework presented by UN Gaza High Representative Nickolay Mladenov. Palestinian media reported one person killed in an IDF drone strike in Khan Younis on March 28, 2026, during the ongoing ceasefire. Three Journalists Killed in Israeli Strike on Press Vehicle in Lebanon.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: All 251 hostages taken on October 7, 2023 are now fully accounted for — 168 returned alive, 85 bodies repatriated, with the last remains (Master Sgt. Ran Gvili) recovered January 26, 2026. Gaza ceasefire Phase Two remains stalled with Hamas refusing full disarmament and Israel refusing withdrawal; the Trump administration's focus has shifted to the Iran-Israel conflict. Israel confirmed $57 billion in economic losses over two years of war while Israel's 2025 GDP recovered to 2.9% growth. The ICC maintained arrest warrants for Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Gallant despite Israel's bid to halt the probe. Eight new daily event files were created covering March 20–27, 2026, including the IDF killing of Hamas commander Kamal Ayash and details of the Board of Peace disarmament plan.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Thu Mar 26, 2026
summary: Al Jazeera reported on March 26, 2026 that details of the 'Board of Peace' plan for Gaza disarmament have been revealed, describing an eight-month, multiphase implementation process. A comprehensive UK Parliamentary investigation documented that 73% of those murdered on October 7, 2023 were civilians — including women, children, elderly, and foreign nationals.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Video circulated on March 25, 2026 showing an Israeli airstrike hitting a displacement tent camp in Deir el-Balah in central Gaza. US DOJ October 7 Terrorism Task Force Falters — Members Fired.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: Israeli forces struck and killed Kamal Ayash, a key Hamas anti-tank unit commander, in a targeted strike on Nuseirat in central Gaza on March 24, 2026. The UN Security Council held a hearing on March 24, 2026 focused on the Gaza situation and the US-backed recovery plan.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: Israel Reports $57 Billion Economic Loss from Two Years of War. The International Criminal Court (ICC) denied Israel's bid to halt the Gaza investigation and maintained arrest warrants for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt reopened on March 19–22, 2026, for limited medical evacuations and returns to Gaza. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu submitted a 55-page document in February 2026 with his answers to the state comptroller's investigation into failures leading to October 7.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: Palestinian weekly reports documented a surge in Israeli settler violence in the West Bank during the Eid period in late March 2026. Hamas Quietly Rebuilding Infrastructure in Gaza Amid Ceasefire.

title: October 7th Attack Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: As of March 20, 2026, the Gaza ceasefire framework established under Trump's 20-point peace plan remains technically in force but deeply fragile. Phase Two of the Gaza ceasefire — declared to have commenced on January 14, 2026 by US envoy Steve Witkoff — remains stalled as of late March 2026.
